Description: Always declare strict_types=1 at the top of every PHP file to prevent implicit type coercion
Do: Put declare(strict_types=1) as the very first statement after <?php
Dont: Rely on PHP's loose type coercion which can cause subtle bugs
Code Good:
<?php declare(strict_types=1);

function add(int $a, int $b): int {
    return $a + $b;
}
add(2, 3);    // OK
add('2', '3'); // TypeError! Caught at compile time
Code Bad:
<?php
// No strict_types
function add(int $a, int $b): int {
    return $a + $b;
}
add('2', '3'); // Returns 5! Silent coercion
add('abc', 3); // Might be 3! PHP coerces 'abc' to 0

Description: Use ?? and ??= to provide default values for nullable expressions concisely
Do: Use ?? for default values. Use ??= for assignment with default
Dont: Write verbose isset() checks or ternary expressions for null handling

Description: Use match instead of switch for strict comparison and expression-based control flow
Do: Use match for value mapping and type-safe comparisons. Match is an expression (returns a value)
Dont: Use switch for simple value mapping which uses loose comparison

Description: Use fn() => for single-expression closures that automatically capture outer variables
Do: Use arrow functions for simple one-expression callbacks in array operations
Dont: Use arrow functions for multi-line logic (not supported)

Description: Use named arguments to improve readability especially for boolean flags and optional parameters
Do: Use named arguments when parameter names add clarity, skip optional params, or with boolean flags
Dont: Pass positional boolean arguments where the meaning is unclear

Description: Use PHP 8.1+ enums for any finite set of values. Backed enums for database storage
Do: Use backed enums for status fields that are stored in the database. Add methods for behavior
Dont: Use string constants, arrays, or class constants for finite value sets

Description: Reference functions and methods as closures using the ... syntax (PHP 8.1+)
Do: Use Closure::fromCallable() or ... syntax to pass methods as callbacks
Dont: Pass method names as strings losing type safety

Description: Return early for edge cases to keep the main logic unindented at the function's top level
Do: Return early for validation failures error cases and edge conditions at the top
Dont: Deeply nest if/else blocks for validation and error handling

Description: Follow PSR-12 extended coding style for consistent readable PHP code
Do: Use PHP CS Fixer or Laravel Pint to enforce PSR-12 automatically
Dont: Invent your own coding style or mix different styles across the project

Description: Use Laravel Collection methods for expressive data transformation pipelines
Do: Chain collection methods for readable data transformations
Dont: Use nested loops and temporary variables for data transformations
Code Good:
$report = $orders
    ->where('status', 'completed')
    ->whereBetween('created_at', [$startDate, $endDate])
    ->groupBy('category')
    ->map(fn($group) => [
        'count' => $group->count(),
        'total' => $group->sum('amount'),
        'average' => $group->avg('amount'),
    ])
    ->sortByDesc('total')
    ->take(10);

Description: Mark properties as readonly to prevent modification after initialization
Do: Use readonly for DTOs value objects and any property that should not change after construction
Dont: Leave properties mutable when they should never change

Description: Use the spread operator (...) for array unpacking variadic parameters and function forwarding
Do: Use ... for combining arrays, forwarding arguments, and variadic params
Dont: Use array_merge() for simple array combining or func_get_args()
Code Bad: mixed ...$args): Model {\n    return Model::create(['name' => $name

Description: Use ?-> to safely access methods and properties on potentially null objects
Do: Use ?-> for optional property/method access chains
Dont: Check for null at every step of a chain

Description: PHP 8.4 property hooks allow defining get/set behavior directly on properties
Do: Use property hooks for computed properties and validation
Dont: Create separate getter/setter methods for simple property access (pre-8.4)

Description: PHP 8.1 Fibers enable cooperative multitasking within PHP. Used internally by frameworks
Do: Use Fibers through framework abstractions (ReactPHP AMPHP RevoltPHP). Avoid creating raw Fibers
Dont: Use raw Fibers directly when a framework provides a cleaner abstraction

Description: Use appropriate string syntax for the complexity level
Do: Use double quotes for simple vars. Heredoc for multi-line. sprintf for formatted output
Dont: Concatenate everything with dots or use sprintf for simple cases
Code Good: // Simple variable:\n$greeting = \Hello

Description: Use CarbonImmutable or DateTimeImmutable for date operations to prevent accidental mutations
Do: Use immutable date objects. Chain operations without modifying the original
Dont: Use mutable DateTime or Carbon which modifies the original object

Description: Store configuration in environment variables. Access via config() helper not env() directly
Do: Use env() only in config files. Access config via config() helper elsewhere
Dont: Call env() directly in application code

Description: Prefer constructor injection over Facades for explicit dependencies and better testability
Do: Inject dependencies via constructor. Use DI in services and controllers
Dont: Use Facades everywhere hiding true dependencies

Description: Convert raw config arrays into typed configuration objects for IDE support and validation
Do: Create typed config classes. Validate on construction
Dont: Access raw config arrays throughout the codebase
